decision.title: Payload + Supabase + Vercel
decision.summary: Use Payload CMS (with-vercel-website template) on Vercel with PostgreSQL hosted on Supabase. Swap template DB env from Neon to Supabase connection string when implementing.
decision.impact: All schema and migrations target Postgres via Payload using `@payloadcms/db-postgres` (node `pg`) for reliable Supabase compatibility; avoid the template's Neon/WebSocket driver against Supabase poolers. Media uses Supabase Storage via `@payloadcms/storage-s3` when env is set; otherwise local media for dev.

decision.title: Where internal process documentation lives
decision.summary: Repo may stay public for transparency if we avoid secrets and customer PII in commits. High-level `.planning/` is OK; detailed SOPs can move to private repo, Notion, or Payload admin-only collections. Customers only see public pages and appropriate forms, not internal quotes or ops tools.
decision.impact: Good-faith, pragmatic process writing; classify sensitive detail out of public git.

decision.title: CRM: EngageBay (validate before production)
decision.summary: Default choice is EngageBay for one shared org, mixed user email domains, marketing, and CRM. Review official API, webhooks, and trial limits in `engagebay-integration-review.md` before locking integrations. HubSpot remains a fallback if API or automation gaps block the roadmap.
decision.impact: Lead sync, sequences, and deals target the CRM abstraction layer with EngageBay active by default; keys stay in env only and provider selection can switch at runtime from the ops dashboard.

decision.title: Marketing site content is seed-first, with quick-edit proof in Payload admin
decision.summary: The launch site should come from repeatable seed content, then allow staff to update reviews, testimonials, before/after proof, and public customer-support pages quickly in Payload admin without touching code.
decision.impact: Homepage, support/legal pages, and core marketing pages remain reproducible across environments through the `pages` collection, while trust content can become an admin-managed collection or global once the quickest acceptable editing path is implemented.

decision.title: Ops dashboard CRM reads are HubSpot-gated
decision.summary: Day-board tasks and open-deal pipeline KPIs on `/ops` call HubSpot only when HubSpot is the active runtime provider and a token is configured. EngageBay remains the default for form submission sync; EngageBay parity for ops-board reads is explicitly out of scope until a later phase.
decision.impact: Internal routes under `src/app/api/internal/hubspot/*` and `src/lib/hubspot/opsClient.ts` implement health, tasks-by-date, and pipeline summary. The portal shows a sync banner and an empty-state message when HubSpot is not active. Form sync continues to use the existing CRM abstraction in `src/lib/crm`.

decision.title: Payload quotes stay the source of truth; EngageBay gets synced deal summaries
decision.summary: Quote detail, pricing logic, and tax notes live in Payload. EngageBay should receive a mirrored deal record for relationship management, follow-up, and pipeline visibility.
decision.impact: Prevents the CRM from becoming the quoting engine while still letting staff manage deal stages, reminders, and customer communication in EngageBay. Write-back from EngageBay to Payload remains a later decision.

decision.title: Quote system: staff-managed, customer-readable for own sent/accepted estimates
decision.summary: Quotes capture job size, description, soiling level, and later economics such as labor, materials, margin, and Texas sales tax per CPA guidance. Staff still manage quote CRUD in Payload, but customers can read their own sent/accepted estimates through the portal when a quote is linked to their account or email.
decision.impact: Quote editing stays internal, while the customer portal can use the same collection as the estimate surface instead of maintaining a second estimate model. `QUOTES_INTERNAL_ENABLED` still gates staff-facing quote-tool shortcuts, not customer read access for their own records.

decision.title: Instant quote math lives in draftable Payload global `quoteSettings`
decision.summary: The public instant quote widget should not depend on hardcoded pricing bands. Service toggles, minimums, square-foot rates, and estimate multipliers live in Payload global `quoteSettings` with drafts enabled, so staff can stage changes and publish them deliberately.
decision.impact: The home-page quote UI and lead submission route read published settings by default, while staff can preview drafts through Payload draft mode. This keeps pricing edits in admin, preserves a publish workflow, and avoids redeploying for normal quote tuning.

decision.title: Business scorecard and growth system belong to phase 06
decision.summary: KPI definitions, projected revenue, MRR, margin review, liabilities, asset ladder, and daily/weekly rhythm are part of advanced ops work. Start them as internal docs/config with real business targets, then promote them to editable Payload data only after schema approval.
decision.impact: Prevents overbuilding too early. The team can use the playbook immediately in docs and dashboard context, while editable ops globals remain a later step once migration work is approved.

decision.title: Internal ops KPI display lives in Payload global `internalOpsSettings`
decision.summary: Admin-editable Internal ops targets global (src/globals/InternalOpsSettings/config.ts) holds display strings for projected revenue and MRR cards plus chart disclaimer and annual revenue goal reference. Access is admin-only. The business momentum chart remains illustrative sample data until real series are integrated (task 06-03).
decision.impact: The /ops route reads this global via Local API with fallbacks if the table is missing; staff edit values in Payload admin, not in code. Portal doc: src/content/docs/ops-dashboard-targets.md (catalog slug ops-dashboard-targets).

decision.title: Liabilities and scorecard definitions in Payload with live dashboard wiring
decision.summary: Operational liability checklist rows live in admin-only collection `ops-liability-items`. Scorecard definition rows (title, formula, target guidance, optional manual value fields, sort order) live in `ops-scorecard-rows`. The portal merges DB rows with defaults from `src/lib/ops/businessOperatingSystem.ts`, seeds those defaults into real records, and now uses scorecard manual values to power KPI cards where appropriate.
decision.impact: The `/ops` dashboard is no longer an empty shell when the collections exist but have no content. Scorecard and liability CRUD now show up in the live dashboard, while HubSpot-backed metrics continue to override projected revenue when active. Customer portal can still be staff-only during rollout by toggling `PORTAL_ADMIN_ONLY=true`.

decision.title: Customer portal is account-first, not docs-first
decision.summary: The customer portal should behave like an account center: estimates, invoices, scheduling, recurring-plan context, and account/contact details. Customers should not be pushed toward docs, the marketing site, Quote settings, or Payload admin from portal navigation.
decision.impact: `/dashboard`, `/estimates`, `/invoices`, `/service-schedule`, and `/account` become the customer surface. Portal docs remain admin-only, and the customer sidebar/navigation strips out marketing/admin clutter.

decision.title: Recurring plan defaults live in draftable Payload global `servicePlanSettings`
decision.summary: Default recurring-plan terms are configurable in Payload global `servicePlanSettings`: two visits per year minimum, 20% off one-off single-job pricing by default, billed across the year in equal installments, with a default six-month cadence. Staff can stage and publish changes instead of hardcoding subscription logic.
decision.impact: The customer portal and service-plan records share one source of truth for recurring-plan defaults. `service-plans` stores per-customer overrides and calculated totals, while the global keeps the baseline commercial policy editable.

decision.title: Payload MCP is the preferred AI editing bridge, but it is migration-gated
decision.summary: Use Payload's official MCP plugin so local editors can read and update approved content surfaces through the same app server. Keep it behind an explicit env flag until the MCP API keys schema is approved and migrated.
decision.impact: Codex and Cursor setup can be documented and prepared in-repo without silently breaking the current database. The endpoint should come from the normal dev server once the migration gate is cleared.

decision.title: Shared passwords and API keys for third-party tools
decision.summary: Requirement acknowledged: team needs one place to view and maintain logins for other systems. Do not store plaintext in content collections without an encryption and security review. Options include a team password manager, Payload encrypted fields plus RBAC, or dedicated secrets patterns.
decision.impact: May remain process plus external tool for v1; in-app vault only after threat model.
